other_observers: DFO Field trip participants ( 16 ) including Charles Thornton Kolbe , Jeannie Mitchell , Richard Prickett , Mary Ellen Olsen , etc; Yes , I am the original finder.
specific_location: 150 yards east of the Sells Lake Trailhead along the Bluff Trail. In the trees and bushes on the cliff face just to the south of the trail.
description: A dendroica type warbler which could be compared in size and jizz to an adjacent adult male Audubon's Warbler . Appeared to have a stouter bill in comparison to the YRWA , complete yellow throat extending to upper breast , lower breast , flanks and undertail coverts white , some faint dark streaking on left side and flanks shows up in photo ( although I did not notice this while observing ) , dark auriculars , pale eye-ring which appeared to be whitish rather than yellow , dark line from eye to base of bill , no pale supercilium or superloral , pale yellowish neck patch ,two pale wing bars on dark wing , tail viewed from below appeared to be damaged or in moult and outer tail feather appeared to be white. Never got a good view of the back as bird was always above our heads.Behaviors: Actively gleaning insects from small bushes and trunk ,branches of a Hackberry tree. In company with flock of Black-capped Chickadees , Juncos , House Finches ,Audubon's Warbler , and Pine Siskins.Call: one hard chip call , responded to playback song.Plumage: immature
photographer: Photographer: John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond, Jeannie Mitchell, Jeannie Mitchell
